strengths: ~15 years of broad full-stack engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Some AI-adjacent exposure via MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling at re:cinq
Track record of technical leadership artifacts: founding GISCollective, conference talks, workshops, and open-source libraries
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML product ownership, research, or platform-building at the depth expected for a Principal AI Strategy role
Title trajectory tops out at Staff/Senior; lacks Principal/Director-level IC scope or evidence of advising strategic customers
No advanced degree in an AI field and no cloud-architect-level depth (AWS CDK usage noted, but no cloud platform strategy work)

key_factors.factor: Depth of AI/ML expertise and shipped AI products
Principal/Director-level IC scope and external influence
Breadth of engineering experience and craftsmanship

response.justification: The role demands recognized AI expertise, Director-level IC scope, and the ability to advise strategic customers on cloud and AI adoption. The résumé shows strong, broad software craftsmanship over ~15 years (re:cinq, Optilyz, Dunnhumby, RIDE), but AI evidence is limited to recent MCP/agent tooling at re:cinq with no shipped AI products, research, or platform leadership. Seniority caps at Staff, with no Principal/Director signals or strategic-customer advisory work. Cloud experience is present (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) but not at platform-strategy depth. Community contributions (FOSS4G talk, workshops, OSS libraries) are positives but don't offset the core AI-strategy gap.
